reviews.text: I am so thankful for to Nura and highly recommend her and her team. She won my case and secured my back pay. She is very professional and knowledgable and answered all my questions promptly. Her knowledge is truly unmatched and she made this stressful time so much easier. Thank you!
Nura is a wonderful lawyer, she is very knowledgeable and kind. My case was difficult, but she was able to win my case. I am so thankful to her and her team!

Edit: I worked specifically with Nura Lufti. I do not recommend this attorney based on my direct experience with her representation in a Social Security Disability claim. She may have opened her own firm 5 months ago per their response, but she has been in practice longer than that and it is very concerning that she is on her own without supervision given my experience.

From the outset, my case was mishandled. During intake, my functional decline and physical inability to work were not properly assessed. Although I explained that I was working only out of survival and necessity, I was never advised that earning above Social Security’s income threshold could result in denial, nor was I informed what that threshold was. As a result, my initial application was denied for excess income, and I ultimately lost approximately 22 months of potential disability back pay. Additionally, why wouldn’t she discuss any help like SSI during the interim? My hands are now deformed and barely functioning because I was ill advised and kept working until my hands were severely injured.

Communication and case management were also deeply problematic. Nura once left a voicemail to return her call then sent an email and letter in the mail threatening withdrawal of representation unless paperwork was completed within 10 days, despite Social Security providing a 60-day deadline with ample time remaining. I completed the paperwork immediately and emailed questions with no response. I continued emailing once a week to follow up again with no response. The final week before the deadline I left a voicemail 3x she finally called me back on Friday and that next Monday was the deadline! I explained this all to Nura, she admitted she had not opened or reviewed any of my prior emails and didn’t apologize. She opened the completed paperwork email with me on the phone — despite it having been sent three weeks earlier!!! She stated she would submit it that day. She did not. The following Monday was the deadline, and my case was subsequently denied for “noncompliance.”

When I raised these concerns, responsibility was deflected, and I was advised to reapply entirely rather than proceed to a hearing, despite my objection that doing so would cause me to lose years of accrued time on my claim. I was also told that appearing before a judge was not desirable, which was concerning, as a hearing is often a critical part of the disability approval process.

This experience resulted in significant delays, multiple denials, and unnecessary harm. My new attorney reviewed all of this information at our very first appointment and clearly explained the process, expectations etc — guidance I had never previously received.

Based on my experience, I strongly encourage others to proceed with caution and seek representation elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse: We take client concerns seriously. Our firm opened in June 2025, and we have no record of this individual ever being a client of our firm. The circumstances described do not relate to any services provided by this business. Due to confidentiality obligations, we cannot address specifics publicly. reviews.ownerResponse:
This client engaged our law firm to handle a relatively simple probate matter. Her mother had passed away, leaving her home to the client and client’s brother. The mother had died several years ago and her estate had not been probated. Client was renting the home to tenants, but now wanted to sell it, and clear the title by probating the estate.

Our firm filed all the necessary documents and paid all the court costs. While the case was on-going client’s brother passed away. In order for us to finish the probate case, a probate case needed to be opened for the brother as his estate now owned his interest in the property. We contacted the client and asked her to contact her brother’s wife to let her know she needed to start a probate case for him. We heard nothing from the wife. Then, (after obtaining her address from the client) we sent a letter to the brother’s wife explaining the situation, and received no response. We later learned the wife, too, had passed away leaving their children sole intestate heirs.

We suggested to client she start a quiet title action to clear title when her brother passed away and an estate had not been opened for him. This is a separate case, completely different from the probate action. The case would most likely be filed in a different court, involved additional legal time/costs and was a more complicated case. We really had no choice but to request an additional fee from the client. She hesitated to pay. In the meantime, her brother’s wife died, adding an additional complication to the case. We again requested the same fee for the same quiet title legal action (her brother’s and wife’s children now have an interest in the property) and the client still has not paid or consented to us filing what needs to be done. This again is an action to quiet title such that the matter can be concluded.

Although, we are saddened the client was dissatisfied with our legal representation, the passing of her brother and his wife created legal complications far beyond the initial probate representation. The client refused to pay the reasonable and necessary legal fees /costs to conclude he matter.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We are very sorry that Ms. Rozell is unhappy with the pace of her father’s Trust and Estate administration; which has been completed. It should be noted that Ms. Rozell is not, and never has been, our client. Our client in this matter was the Personal Representative of the Estate, and Trustee of the Trust.

Unfortunately, the Will and Trust were not as “straight forward” as Ms. Rozell believes. There were numerous assets from numerous entities to be discovered, obtained, and distributed. Some were more difficult than others to obtain from the entities holding them.

Ms. Rozell was a minor when the administration began, and was not familiar with the process of probating a Will or administering a Trust, and did not know the complications that can occur. This was actually one of the more difficult estates we have had to deal with, and the difficulties we had prying loose assets from mutual funds and former employers were very frustrating for us, as well as the beneficiaries. We did succeed in obtaining all of the assets and distributing them to the beneficiaries, but not as quickly as we would have liked. We sincerely apologize that we were not able to do this as quickly as Ms. Rozell wished as well.

reviews.text: I called about a landlord/tenant situation I'm having and was told since I'm the tenant there's nothing they can do because they only deal with landlords..that i would have to find another attorney and hung up on me. How rude can you be? At least refer me to someone that can help me
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Beth, I’m sorry if we were abrupt with you. We do only work with landlords, but we certainly do not mean to be rude when letting you know that. It can be difficult to find a private attorney who represents tenants because many tenants cannot afford their fees. Therefore, they are mostly served by legal aid offices. We are in the process of compiling a list of referral attorneys for just such this situation but we, also, have not had much luck in finding private attorneys who take on tenants as clients. Here, however, is a list of some of the local legal aid offices:

Detroit Free Legal Aid Clinic, Inc. 313-833-0058
Legal Aid and Defender Association 313-967-5555
Neighborhood Legal Services Michigan 313-964-1975
The William Booth Legal Aid Clinic 313- 361-6340

Best of luck to you and, again, our sincere apologies.

- John Keenan

The attorneys at Legal Supreme have a deep understanding of personal injury law, and their experience was clear in the way they handled negotiations with insurance companies. Rather, I felt like she had my best interest in mind, focusing on solving the problem as resourcefully as possible. She is an exceptionally talented attorney, and having the good fortune to find her help was itself the silver lining. From “Angry Black Man” Narrative to Civil Rights Posturing: My Experience with Amir Makled!

As the victim in a criminal case, I watched attorney Amir I. Makled shift his story depending on who was listening. Three episodes stand out.

1. Preliminary exam: Painting me as the aggressor
At the preliminary hearing before a white judge, Mr. Makled implied that I was the one spoiling for a fight. When I testified that the assailant called me “abed” (“slave” in Arabic), he quickly moved on and focused on how I reacted. In his closing, he argued that “two gentlemen can agree to fight,” likened the confrontation to a duel, and suggested the altercation was mutual. The racial insult and my trauma were almost dismissed; instead, I felt cast as the “angry Black man” who wanted to fight for his honor.

2. Bench trial: Denying the slur and playing civil rights advocate
Months later, at the bench trial before a Black judge, Mr. Makled’s tone changed. He questioned me about the same slur, calling it “a very disgusting and derogatory word,” but then drilled me on whether I was sure it was said. He asked why I reacted the way I did and framed my testimony as my “contention”. In closing, he acknowledged I had alleged a racial slur but insisted there were no threats and portrayed the encounter as two men mutually choosing to fight. To me, it felt like he was using civil‑rights language to appear empathetic while still denying the reality of the slur to manage the judge’s reaction.

3. Truth prevails despite dishonest tactics
Throughout the case, Mr. Makled’s tactics felt dishonest. During cross‑examination he held the personal protection order in his hand and forced me to read only select lines, refusing to let me examine it. He later chose not to enter the document into evidence. Yet in the end, none of his shifting narratives mattered. His client was convicted of aggravated assault, proving that the truth, despite his racial games and document omission games, could not be buried.

I filled the bankruptcy based on the fact I would be able to recover much easier from it because I have absolutely NO INTENTION of giving up my car or home.

Come to find out my bankruptcy was granted and Babut Law offices failed to respond to the reaffirmation agreement for my mortgage and autoloan. This being my first time filling for such an unfortunate situation, I didn't know there was additional forms that had to be signed and filled out otherwise I would have been on them. I PAID this firm to do the job they said they would do.

Babut law blamed it on their paralegal not sending me the forms to sign to return to their creditors even though they never even checked to make sure everything was done accordingly with my case on the issue. I was just notified today by William Babut that there is nothing they can do to fix their error so my mortgage and my autoloan was completely dropped from my credit report so those no longer count on building my credit.

I now have to go outside my means and find a credit card or another loan which is not what I wanted to do because of their error. This should have never happened...

Do NOT go here. They will tell you what you want to hear to get your $1100 and then just say they they messed up but at least you can walk away from your car or house if you want to...even though you were told differently when you went in 2 times for a consultation.

This is your life. Find an attorney that follows through on what you ask of them. It is your life that will be affected and your credit you will have to work on. You're just money and forms to this place, they don't care.

Their defense was that not every attorney does reaffirmation of certain debts, HOWEVER that was a service THEY advertised to me. Otherwise I could have found another attorney.

I would never recommend this firm to anyone.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Ms Barrett.
Thank you for sharing how you’re feeling. We’re genuinely sorry that this experience has been so upsetting, and we understand how overwhelming these situations can be.

Throughout your case, our team took time to explain your options and the consequences associated with each one, including the reaffirmation agreement on the vehicle. While we strongly advised against reaffirming, that decision is ultimately the client’s to make, and we respect your right to choose the path you felt was best at the time.

Your Chapter 7 case was successfully completed, and all eligible debts were discharged, which provided the relief the bankruptcy process allows. Issues that arise after a case has closed—such as vehicle problems or lender actions tied to a reaffirmed loan—are unfortunately outside the scope of what bankruptcy can prevent or control.

We truly regret that you’re feeling disappointed, and we wish you nothing but the best moving forward.
Thank you Christopher for taking the time to leave a review. If you have any concerns, please feel free to reach out directly at mike@fairmaxlaw.com.

reviews.text: Frank was incredibly professional and thorough. I hired Frank to assist me in having the state of Michigan release a "hold" on my license. Even with the hurdles posed by Covid, changes to the court system and the postal service, Frank was able to help me navigate the courts and successfully obtain my license. Frank spent a lot of time looking through my documents and ensuring everything was in order before submitting, and although it was a lot of work, it alleviated stress during the actual submission process. 10/10 highly recommend!

This was a three-year process in the making. Krystle, Laura, and the rest of the staff treated us like family when helping bring my grandfather to the United States. The entire staff is bilingual, making them a great choice for anyone whose first language is Spanish. When things were going against us, K-M Law quickly provided a blanket of support and were ready to walk through the fire with us. During this process, we had dozens of meetings. KM Law was super flexible with meeting times. We chose our in-person meetings while we were in the United States. Krystle and her staff met with us through phone visits and email while we were in Ciudad Juarez, Mexico. Krystle and her staff were very responsive and prompt, especially with the U.S. Embassy giving us less than 24 hours’ notice to respond, scan, upload, and provide the required forms and documentation. Additionally, they also helped with scanning and uploading documentation, especially since we were in an area of Mexico with poor internet and phone reception and speed. Our phone calls kept dropping, but Krystle and her staff didn't give up, and Krystle personally called us and walked us through every step. She was very kind, very thorough, and very upfront with pricing. Her entire staff is readily available. They go out of their way to help you. The best part is when our dream came true and we were able to thank them for all their hard work.

Very disappointing experience. I am writing this review because my brother doesn’t have a google account so please don’t say it’s not existing client because you know us very well.
We hired Ahmed Elsharnoby to handle my brother’s divorce. He assured us that he had handled many divorce cases before and that we had nothing to worry about. However, when the court date arrived, he seemed extremely unprepared and nervous — it honestly felt like it was his first time handling such a case.

My brother later hired a different law firm, and even that attorney was surprised that Ahmed had taken on a divorce case given his lack of experience. The new attorney had to fix everything and continue the case properly. The attorney told the judge of how Ahmed has no experience and he’s not supposed to handle and take cases like this. We were very disappointed and felt misled after paying full fees for poor representation.

Would not recommend for divorce or family law matters.

I had a very frustrating experience with this law office from the very beginning. The secretary who answers the phone is extremely rude and unhelpful — she acts like you’re bothering her every time you call.

I made an appointment and waited a whole week for it. When the day finally came, I called to confirm, only to be told the lawyer was “in court” and too busy to talk. They said he would call the next day — he didn't. I called again, and the same excuse came up. Then they promised he would call that night — still nothing. The next day, they pushed it again until next week.

At that point, I explained that my situation was urgent, but they clearly didn’t care. They kept making promises to call me back, and never once followed through. The lack of communication, organization, and respect for clients’ time is unbelievable.

If this is how they treat potential clients before even starting a case, I can’t imagine how they handle things afterwards. Save yourself the stress and look for a law office that actually values ​​professionalism and basic courtesy.

My experience with this law firm was extremely frustrating from the start. The employee (secretary) who answered the phone was extremely rude and uncooperative, making you feel like you were bothering her every time you called.

They said they would call me the next day—and they didn't. I called again, and they repeated the same excuse. They then promised to call me back in the evening—and nothing happened. The next day, they postponed the appointment again until next week.

At this point, I explained that my situation was urgent and couldn't be postponed, but they showed no interest. They kept promising to call and never followed through. The level of communication, organization, and respect for clients' appointments is extremely poor.

If this is how they treat clients before they even start a case, I can't imagine what their treatment will be like afterward. I would advise anyone to avoid this office and look for a place that values ​​respect and true professionalism.

Truly one of the most unprofessional law offices I’ve ever dealt with and that’s saying something. I paid $160 upfront for a legal consultation in early April. The consultation never happened. What followed was a month and a half-long circus of excuses, missed follow-ups, and borderline incoherent communication from multiple branches of the firm (New York, Michigan, and Dearborn).

After reaching out repeatedly and being bounced between lawyers and office staff who seemed to barely know what was going on, I was promised a refund “immediately” on at least three separate occasions. Spoiler: it wasn’t. Even when I sent my bank info (as requested), it took over six weeks and multiple chase-up emails before I finally received the refund via Zelle — with zero notice or confirmation. Not even a “we’ve sent it” message. Just silence, then money.

When I requested deletion of my personal data, a basic privacy expectation, especially after an unused consultation, I was met with conflicting statements like: “you don’t have a file” and “we’re keeping it for records.” Right. Which is it?

The overall tone from the office ranged from passive-aggressive to outright dismissive. At one point, I was even told to “have your lawyer contact us” because apparently being a paying customer wasn’t enough to warrant answers.

If you’re looking for basic professionalism, responsiveness, or any sense of ethical responsibility, look elsewhere. This place operates more like a disorganized family business than a law firm, and unfortunately, they seem to think that’s acceptable, maybe within their community. Finally, it’s surprising how out of 5 staff members I talked to, including the primary lawyer, only one spoke English decently.

But hey, if you enjoy scavenger hunts for your own refund and Kafkaesque customer service with a side of attitude, this firm might just be your perfect match

I cannot recommend this attorney highly enough. He stepped in during a very serious legal matter and handled everything with incredible efficiency, professionalism, and care. From the very beginning, he did his due diligence, explained every step clearly, and made sure I fully understood my options and the strategy moving forward.

What stood out most was how confident and secure he made me feel throughout the entire process. Legal situations can be overwhelming, but his calm, thoughtful approach and responsiveness made all the difference. The matter was resolved effectively and far more quickly than I anticipated.

If you’re looking for an attorney who is not only highly competent but also genuinely invested in your peace of mind, look no further. In matters like these, you hope it’s a one-time experience—but if I ever need legal representation again, he is second to none, and I would not hesitate to recommend him to others.

Outstanding experience with Berri Azakir from start to finish. Berri was incredibly prompt, kind, and discreet, and spent a significant amount of time walking me through my case, explaining my options, and involving me in the overall strategy. I felt heard, respected, and well-informed at every step.

His staff was equally professional and helpful, making communication seamless and stress-free. Special recognition is owed to Hana, who was an absolute pleasure to work with. My case was handled efficiently, thoughtfully, and with the utmost professionalism. I would not hesitate to return for any future legal needs and highly recommend Berri Azakir and the entire team. If you are looking for an attorney who is not only highly competent but also genuinely cares about their clients, I suggest you give them a call!

reviews.text:
​If you are looking for a defense team that actually delivers on their promises, look no further than Mourad Law. From the moment they took my case, I knew I was in capable hands.
​Here is why I give them my highest recommendation:
​Knowledge & Expertise: They are incredibly sharp and clearly know the law inside and out. You can tell they have the respect of the court and a deep understanding of legal strategy.
​Top-Tier Communication: I was never left wondering what was happening with my case. They kept me informed every step of the way, which took a huge weight off my shoulders.
​Total Dedication: They take your case very seriously. It didn’t feel like I was just another file on a desk; they genuinely fought for the best possible outcome for me.
​Stress-Free Process: The best part about working with them is the peace of mind. They handled 100% of the heavy lifting. All I had to do was show up to court, and they took care of everything else.
​The Bottom Line: If you need a defense attorney who is reliable, professional, and gets the job done, I 100% recommend Mourad Law to anyone. They are the team
Had one of the best experience today with my brother Raed Mourad he got my case dismissed and is highly intelligent with his work and loves helping you win!.

Your lawyer,

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Abdul,

Our staff’s response was consistent with our office policy and standard legal practice. Questions about our case experience and legal services are directed to an attorney consultation so they can be answered accurately and in the proper context.

Immigration matters, especially asylum cases, are serious and fact specific, and we do not provide partial information or discuss our legal caseload through brief phone inquires.

Scheduling a consultation to speak directly with an attorney is standard practice in the legal profession and ensures people receive proper guidance rather than incomplete information.

We wish you the best moving forward.

reviews.text:
Today's Date: Wednesday January 28, 2026

Updated from previous posted review on Sunday January 25, 2026:

Mr. Hammond, the owner/Senior Partner of Mourad Law, called me on Monday January 26, 2026 regarding my Review I posted on Sunday January 25 regarding his firm and his Secretary Ms. Smith. He apologized for any miscommunication,etc and I also apologized for the review. I wasn't trying to cause any trouble, but I just wanted him to help me because he does have a great reputation of helping his clients.

So he indicated he would call his secretary to set up a time for the next day. She, Ms. Smith called and scheduled my free consultation for Tuesday January 27 at 2 pm.

I arrived around 1:15 pm. He came around within 15 minutes and he was nice enough to see me early. My appointment was at 2 pm, but I came earlier because of the weather and I was on the bus.

We spoke about my case regarding the Citation and I presented him with the evidence (phone records via FOIA, audio conversations via FOIA, video, emails) to dispute the false police report. He indicated that this was BS and not right.

His law firm seems to have a very sharp team of attorneys and administrative staff. I also apologized to his secretary, Ms. Smith too. She was SUPER NICE and his other Secretary was nice enough to make me some hot chocolate. It was good too. It's also a lovely and professional looking law practice.

He promised me a reasonable retainer fee. I'm not sure if I can pay that. If it goes to trial, his services are $350 an hour. Since I only receive SSD, he indicated he would work with me.

I just want the truth to come out regarding the false police report. The Court Appointed Attorney that I have seems disinterested, unresponsive, and hasn't confirmed she reviewed my evidence or informed me of her defenses. She indicated she doesn't want to rock the boat with the Prosecution.

Mr Hammond isn't afraid to do that. That's what a Defense Attorney is about rocking the boat to obtain justice for their clients. He was caring and most importantly he believed I was telling the truth based on the evidence I showed and played for him. Mr. Hammond said he would reach out to my Court Appointed Attorney as well so she could fight for me. He knows of her and indicated she has to fight this false charge against me.

After we spoke about everything, he was nice enough to call me an Uber to get home. I told him he didn't have to.

But I really APPRECIATED it because I was exhausted and didn't feel like taking 3 buses home😅

He said if he knew I was coming by 3 buses, he would send an Uber for me. I told him that's okay, it was worth coming to his office regarding the consultation. But it meant a lot to me that he cared especially since it was 11 degrees that day.

He also was nice enough to let me have a bracelet saying, Palestine. He knew I was admiring it and asked him where he got it from. I told him I couldn't take that because it was symbolic. He said there's plenty more like it. See pic.

This morning, Wednesday, January 28, 2026, he called me to follow up regarding reaching out to my Court Appointed Attorney. He indicated that he left her a message to call me regarding my concerns about my case. But unfortunately no response from her.

The point is that I misjudged the law office of Mourad Law. The owner, Mr Mohammed Ali Hammond, felt bad if I misinterpreted something from his staff and he wanted to correct any misunderstandings.

I also apologized. I can be overly sensitive at times.

I'm not sure what I should do though. I just want someone that I can feel comfortable with representing me. I want to know what their defenses and tactics are going to be in the Court room.

Mr Hammond is ready to take action and that's what I want in a Criminal Defense Attorney, someone that isn't afraid to take the Prosecutor on & prove their client is innocent.

He was generous and sympathetic more than my court appointed Attorney. He did give me the opportunity to come into his office. So I was grateful about that and we patched things up😊

Impeccable Service

During the course of my divorce I was repeatedly falsely accused by my ex-husband of aggravated stalking and violating a personal protection order. All of his acts were solely based on lies to avoid paying child support. My ex-husband had previously lied about being assaulted just months before. Long story short there were alleged  violations of probation and I was eventually placed on a tether and under house arrest for months.

My case was assigned and reviewed by 3 court appointed attorneys one of which did not appear on my behalf. After being failed, I was determined to prove my innocence.  After a more thorough independent search, I discovered Attorney Chris Kessel at Chris Kessel Law in Detroit, MI.

Chris allowed me to be involved in every aspect of my case from the first call to the resolution. Being homebound, Attorney Kessel was able to interview me at my home and sort through my evidence with a fine-toothed comb. Chris was readily available to answer all of my questions and responded quickly to assist with any issues that came up, even after the case was closed.

Attorney Kessel was able to get an expert forensic company to examine my devices  and testify to their findings. After presenting our evidence along with expert testimony the jury returned with a NOT GUILTY verdict on all charges.

With 12yrs of experience, Chris demonstrated excellent courtroom etiquette. In addition, he exhibited compassion, assertiveness and good judgement. Chris Kessel Law is the definition of what a law firm should be. I definitely recommend Chris Kessel Law for all of your legal matters.

Highly recommend!
Chris is knowledgeable, helpful, and encouraging every step of the way.

Chris is very professional and once I retained him all my worries left. He responds fast and I'm happy that everything was taken care off through email and text.

HE'S A BAD MAN, when it comes to that court room. I had a very tough case which would had made me guilty from he say, she say. But the evidence just wasn't there. Chris worked hard from the time I contacted him til my verdict day to stress the issue of reasonable doubt. Along with having a new born son at home which took away from his sleep. On March 15th, 2018 I was found NOT GUILTY of 2 felonies and 1 misdemeanor. "If it's the truth you don't have to remember it", in My Kessel Voice lol.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Abe, thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We genuinely value every review we receive, as it allows us to learn, grow, and continue improving the services we provide to our community.

Whenever it’s convenient for you, please feel free to contact us or stop by the office so we can discuss your concerns directly. We are committed to addressing any issues you experienced and to ensuring that every client leaves with greater confidence in our work.

Thank you again for your honesty, and we hope to have the chance to speak with you soon.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your review. We want to take this opportunity to clarify that shortly after you left our firm for another, we received notice that our work on your case resulted in an approval. While you may believe that the outcome was due to the efforts of your new firm, we are confident that the approval was a direct result of the years of work we completed on your behalf.

That said, we understand that you may choose to give credit elsewhere, and we accept that. Your right to leave a review, even a one-star review, is something we respect. What matters most to us is that you ultimately received the positive outcome you deserved. Whether or not we receive acknowledgment, we take pride in knowing that our efforts contributed to your success.

We wish you all the best in the future and remain genuinely happy for you.

Sincerely,
HLG
